app.title: Game Fence — Time-based blocking
intro.line1: Limits launching during the windows you define: matching processes are closed automatically.
intro.line21: On startup the app stays hidden (no taskbar or notification area icon);
intro.line22: use the global shortcut {hotkey} to show this window.
intro.line3: Closing with × hides it again; monitoring keeps running.
msg.keyboard_body:
Install the package:

  pip install keyboard

to use the shortcut {hotkey} (the only way to open the UI — the app starts hidden).
msg.selection_body: Select a rule in the list.
msg.dialog_open_body: Close the open dialog first (rule editor, etc.).
rule.title: Blocking rule
rule.ok_hint: OK = save this rule to the list
rule.hint: Example: Mon/Tue/Thu = ‘Blocked all day’; other days ‘Access allowed only between…’ and the time ranges when the app may run (outside that, closure is possible).
rule.legend: [0] Executable blocked all day. [1] Blocked outside the range (end time exclusive; 24:00 allowed). [2] This rule does not apply that day. [3] Legacy: block only during the window.
rule.mode_1: 1 — Access allowed only between… (otherwise may be closed)
msg.rule_extension_body: The file does not end in .exe. Continue?
msg.rule_interval_missing: For {weekday}, enter start and end times.
msg.rule_order_invalid: Start time must be before end ({weekday}).
msg.rule_all_none: All rows are ‘no closure’ — this rule will do nothing.
